methods.description: This method describes the collection, rearing, and hypoxia challenge of P. akamusi larvae to study their physiological and genetic responses under hypoxic conditions.

steps.description: The larvae of P. akamusi were collected with a Surber sampler (30 × 30 cm, 500 µm mesh) in the Taihu Lake basin.
steps.searchable_summary: collect P. akamusi larvae using Surber sampler

steps.description: The viable P. akamusi were reared on an artificial diet of fish flake food in culture boxes containing 2 L of dechlorinated tap water and 1-2 cm of acid-washed sand under 25 ± 1°C temperature conditions.
steps.searchable_summary: rear P. akamusi larvae in controlled conditions

steps.description: After 3 days of domestication, robust, large, and color-coordinated 4th instar larvae were selected for the experiment.
steps.searchable_summary: select robust 4th instar larvae for experiment

steps.description: A hypoxic experimental group and a control group with four biological replicates each were established.
steps.searchable_summary: establish hypoxic and control groups

steps.description: Larvae were transferred to 250 mL glass beakers containing 200 mL of aerated dechlorinated tap water (90 Larvae per beaker).
steps.searchable_summary: transfer larvae to glass beakers

steps.description: In hypoxic groups, DO was controlled by bubbling nitrogen gas through the water tanks, and the oxygen content was checked by a DO meter to ensure it did not exceed 0.5 mg/L.
steps.searchable_summary: control DO in hypoxic groups using nitrogen gas

steps.description: Two larvae in each biological replicate were selected and washed in ultrapure water, then fixed in glass vials containing 4% Paraformaldehyde Fix Solution for HE staining at 96 h.
steps.searchable_summary: fix larvae for HE staining

steps.description: Six whole P. akamusi larvae were selected from each biological replicate for physiological indicators at 0 h, 12 h, 24 h, 48 h, 72 h, and 96 h.
steps.searchable_summary: select larvae for physiological indicators

steps.description: Six larvae in each biological replicate were selected for transcriptome and metabolome analyses at 96 h.
steps.searchable_summary: select larvae for transcriptome and metabolome analyses

methods.description: This method outlines the process of isolating RNA, constructing cDNA libraries, and sequencing to analyze gene expression patterns in P. akamusi larvae under hypoxic stress.

steps.description: Utilizing the TRIzol reagent, total RNA was isolated from P. akamusi larvae.
steps.searchable_summary: isolate total RNA using TRIzol reagent

steps.description: Total RNA quantity and integrity were assessed using the Agilent Bioanalyzer 2100 equipment.
steps.searchable_summary: assess RNA quality with Agilent Bioanalyzer

steps.description: Each sample was chosen to have 1.5 g of high-quality RNA for the investigation.
steps.searchable_summary: select high-quality RNA samples

steps.description: The mRNA Seq preparation sample kit instructions were followed to construct the final cDNA library utilizing reverse transcription.
steps.searchable_summary: construct cDNA library using mRNA Seq kit

steps.description: On the Illumina HiSeq platform, paired-end DNA sequencing was carried out according to the vendor's suggested technique.
steps.searchable_summary: perform paired-end sequencing on Illumina HiSeq

steps.description: Clean data (clean reads) were chosen from the raw data (raw reads) to explore the patterns of gene expression.
steps.searchable_summary: filter raw reads to obtain clean reads

steps.description: The sequence quality was checked using FastQC.
steps.searchable_summary: check sequence quality with FastQC

steps.description: With Trinity 2.4.0, clean data were then put together into a transcriptome for reference.
steps.searchable_summary: assemble transcriptome using Trinity

steps.description: The Basic Local Alignment Search Tool (BLAST) was used for transcriptome annotation.
steps.searchable_summary: annotate transcriptome using BLAST

steps.description: The R package edgeR was used to identify the differentially expressed Unigenes with statistical significance.
steps.searchable_summary: identify differentially expressed genes using edgeR

methods.description: This method involves performing GO and KEGG enrichment analyses to understand the biological processes and pathways affected by differentially expressed genes in P. akamusi under hypoxic stress.

steps.description: GO annotation enrichment was performed to analyze differentially expressed genes in P. akamusi under hypoxic stress.
steps.searchable_summary: perform GO enrichment analysis

steps.description: KEGG annotation enrichment was used to analyze the differentially expressed genes in P. akamusi under hypoxic stress.
steps.searchable_summary: perform KEGG enrichment analysis

methods.description: This method describes the KEGG enrichment analysis of differentially accumulated metabolites in P. akamusi related to acute hypoxic stress.

steps.description: KEGG annotation enrichment was used to analyze the differentially accumulated metabolites in P. akamusi related to acute hypoxic stress.
steps.searchable_summary: perform KEGG enrichment analysis on metabolites

methods.method_name: Integrative Analysis of Transcriptome and Metabolome
methods.description: This method involves an integrative analysis of the transcriptome and metabolome to identify correlations between gene expression and metabolite abundance in P. akamusi under hypoxic stress.

steps.description: The Pearson coefficient of gene expression and metabolite abundance was calculated to evaluate the correlation between genes and metabolites.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate Pearson correlation between genes and metabolites

steps.description: The correlation matrix of the heat map revealed the positive and negative correlations between metabolites and genes.
steps.searchable_summary: visualize correlations with heat map
